name: fragile-lives
description: Stephen Westaby's Fragile Lives — surgical memoir toolkit exploring the mindset of a world-class heart surgeon, the reality of cardiac surgery, and the human cost of pushing medical boundaries
useCase: Understand the surgeon's mindset, learn how innovation happens in medicine, cope with high-stakes decisions, and find meaning in life-or-death work
knownRisks: The book contains graphic descriptions of surgery that may disturb some readers
Westaby's views on medical bureaucracy are his personal opinions, not universally held
The memoir is one surgeon's perspective — not necessarily representative of cardiac surgery generally
